figures.caption: Figure 2. Qualitative comparison of 3D head avatar creation and animation on challenging texture cases. Our mesh-based framework successfully models high-fidelity texture details with only 8K vertices, substantially outperforming the Gaussian-based LAM method that requires 80K Gaussian points while still failing to capture fine details.
Figure 9. Qualitative comparison with more baselines.

Figure 1. Overall Framework. Our method reconstructs an animatable 3D texture head mesh from a single image through dual shape and texture branches. After extracting features from the input image with a shared transformer, the shape branch predicts vertex deformations while the texture branch synthesizes UV texture maps, both conditioned on a FLAME template. Both branches are refined iteratively via GRU decoders with topology correction for shape refinement and reprojection guidance for texture enhancement.

Figure 7. Reconstructed geometry and texture map visualization. Our method produces a mesh with high-fidelity texture, even when animated to an expression different from the input image. The sharp, consistently unwrapped texture details demonstrate the effectiveness of our dual-branch design.
